answer: task_shape=single specified implementation leaf; executor=subagent; effect_floor=T1. Both exact targets are already T1-qualified and admitted. Select A for lower latency while its tight capacity remains valid, with B as the ample same-floor fallback when A cannot reserve or launch; never lower the floor. Create the runtime, obtain a real session/process handle, bind and link it, then move the task to in_flight. Exit zero and self-reported green tests terminalize the runtime agent but do not complete the task. The orchestrator independently checks workspace, diff, tests, artifact and acceptance; only passing evidence produces done+verified. Failure keeps the task active and may trigger the qualified T1 fallback or replan.
